title: Padanilam Parabrahma Temple
tagline: Padanilam Parabrahma Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Padanilam Parabrahma Temple (പടനിലം പരബ്രഹ്മ ക്ഷേത്രം ) is situated at Padanilam in Mavelikara taluk of Alappuzha district in Kerala, India.
overview.description: Padanilam Parabrahma Temple (പടനിലം പരബ്രഹ്മ ക്ഷേത്രം ) is situated at Padanilam in Mavelikara taluk of Alappuzha district in Kerala, India. It is one of the major temples in erstwhile Travancore state. Padanilam is the cultural center of Nooranad region. The temple is situated 17 km east of Kayamkulam and 7 km south west of Pandalam.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Padanilam
topPlaces.description: Padanilam is a town located in Onattukara region of Alappuzha district, Kerala. The word Padanilam means "land of battles" (pada means "battle" and nilam means "land").

topPlaces.name: Nooranad
topPlaces.description: Nooranad (also anglicized as Noornad or Nooranadu) is a developing town in Mavelikkara taluk of Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located at a distance of 8 Km south-west of Pandalam, in Kayamkulam route.

topPlaces.name: Palamel
topPlaces.description: Palamel is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located 10 km south of Pandalam.

topPlaces.name: Charummood
topPlaces.description: Charummood (also spelled Charummoodu) is a town in Mavelikkara Taluk of Alappuzha district in Kerala, India. Charummood is 12 km east of the nearest town Kayamkulam, 15 km west of Adoor and 12 km south of Mavelikkara.

topPlaces.name: Chunakkara
topPlaces.description: Chunakkara is a village in Mavelikara Tahsil of Alappuzha district, in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located on the Kollam Theni Highway spanning in around 6 km (3.7 mi) in length along the highway.

topPlaces.name: Edappon
topPlaces.description: Edappon is a small town situated in Nooranad panchayat in Alappuzha district of the state of Kerala in South India. It is on the bank of the Achankovil river.

topPlaces.name: Pandalam Bridge
topPlaces.description: Pandalam Bridge, popularly known as Kurunthottayam Bridge, is a concrete bridge constructed at Pandalam junction in Kerala, India. Kerala Public Works Department commissioned the bridge and opened for public at December 2016.

topPlaces.name: Kulanada
topPlaces.description: Kulanada is a village in the district of Pathanamthitta bordering with Alappuzha district in Kerala state within India. Kulanada assumed its name from the combination of words "Kulam" and "Nada".

items.note: Padanilam is a town located in Onattukara region of Alappuzha district, Kerala. The word Padanilam means "land of battles" (pada means "battle" and nilam means "land").

Nooranad (also anglicized as Noornad or Nooranadu) is a developing town in Mavelikkara taluk of Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located at a distance of 8 Km south-west of Pandalam, in Kayamkulam route.
Palamel is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located 10 km south of Pandalam.

Charummood (also spelled Charummoodu) is a town in Mavelikkara Taluk of Alappuzha district in Kerala, India. Charummood is 12 km east of the nearest town Kayamkulam, 15 km west of Adoor and 12 km south of Mavelikkara.

Chunakkara is a village in Mavelikara Tahsil of Alappuzha district, in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located on the Kollam Theni Highway spanning in around 6 km (3.7 mi) in length along the highway.
Edappon is a small town situated in Nooranad panchayat in Alappuzha district of the state of Kerala in South India. It is on the bank of the Achankovil river.

Pandalam Bridge, popularly known as Kurunthottayam Bridge, is a concrete bridge constructed at Pandalam junction in Kerala, India. Kerala Public Works Department commissioned the bridge and opened for public at December 2016.
Kulanada is a village in the district of Pathanamthitta bordering with Alappuzha district in Kerala state within India. Kulanada assumed its name from the combination of words "Kulam" and "Nada".

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Padanilam Parabrahma Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Padanilam Parabrahma Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Padanilam Parabrahma Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (85 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (86 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Padanilam Parabrahma Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Padanilam Parabrahma Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Padanilam Parabrahma Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Padanilam Parabrahma Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Padanilam Parabrahma Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Padanilam, Nooranad, Palamel, Charummood, Chunakkara.
